     The Numbers Business Report

Father Stu (2022)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $21,096,678Details
International Box Office $892,744
Worldwide Box Office $21,989,422
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,306,534
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,053,396
Total $2,359,930
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When an injury ends his amateur boxing career, Stuart Long moves to L.A. dreaming of stardom. While scraping by as a supermarket clerk, he meets Carmen, a Catholic Sunday school teacher who seems immune to his bad-boy charm. Determined to win her over, the longtime agnostic starts going to church to impress her. But surviving a terrible motorcycle accident leaves him wondering if he can use his second chance to help others find their way, leading to the surprising realization that he is meant to be a Catholic priest. Despite a devastating health crisis and the skepticism of Church officials and his estranged parents, Stu pursues his vocation with courage and compassion, inspiring not only those closest to him but countless others along the way.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$5,402,642 (25.6% of total gross)
Legs:3.90 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:95.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$4,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,705 opening theaters/2,705 max. theaters, 4.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $23,961,657

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 13th, 2022 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
December 9th, 2022 (Wide) by Sony Pictures, released as Father Stu: Reborn
International Releases: April 21st, 2022 (Limited) (New Zealand)
May 12th, 2022 (Wide) (Australia)
May 13th, 2022 (Wide), released as El milagro del padre Stu (Spain)
May 13th, 2022 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
May 19th, 2022 (Wide) (Mexico)
Video Release: May 31st, 2022 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language throughout.
(Rating bulletin 2720 (cert #53571), 2/23/2022)
PG-13 for language, an accident scene, sexual references, some violence and smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2756 (cert #54109) (Edited Version), 11/2/2022)
Running Time: 124 minutes
Filming location (city or town): Los Angeles, CA
Plot point: Boxing, Christians, Pastor
Subgenre: Faith-Based Drama
Target audience: Faith-Based Film
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, CJ Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

When an injury ends his amateur boxing career, Stuart Long moves to L.A. dreaming of stardom. While scraping by as a supermarket clerk, he meets Carmen, a Catholic Sunday school teacher who seems immune to his bad-boy charm. Determined to win her over, the longtime agnostic starts going to church to impress her. But surviving a terrible motorcycle accident leaves him wondering if he can use his second chance to help others find their way, leading to the surprising realization that he is meant to be a Catholic priest. Despite a devastating health crisis and the skepticism of Church officials and his estranged parents, Stu pursues his vocation with courage and compassion, inspiring not only those closest to him but countless others along the way.
